This problem is famously solved using a Trie (Prefix Tree) + DFS (Backtracking).
Insert all given words into a Trie. This allows you to quickly check if a sequence of characters on the board could potentially form a valid word.
Iterate over every cell in the board. Perform a DFS from that cell, keeping track of the current node in the Trie.
To avoid visiting the same cell twice in the same word, temporarily mark it with a special character like '#' and restore it after the DFS call returns.
